data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file to disk but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild compilation items are already collected by the time `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file exists on disk but isn't included in the compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>`.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the MSBuild `Compile` item group. By the time `CoreCompile` runs, the list of files to compile is already frozen — so the generated file was ignored by Roslyn.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>` in the target, which dynamically includes the generated file in compilation.
